## 3. Priorities & Struggles

**What he said matters most (his own words, highest weight):**
> "It's not about the games I develop today. It's not about any of my dozens of projects. It's about what I'm going to start doing seriously in 6 months. Right now, it's only about understanding what's happening, understanding what's worth pursuing and what is not. And I have no idea. That's why I'm trying so many things. I need to be at the edge."

This is the defining signal. Julien is in a deliberate reconnaissance phase — not executing, but observing. The quiet nights are not drift; they are part of the method. The 6-month horizon (roughly Oct 2026) is when he expects to commit to something real.

**What's hard:**
- Uncertainty is the stated condition, not a temporary state. He has no idea what's worth pursuing — that's the honest read.
- Many parallel projects (Grassroot Hoppers, blog, guitar, dancing, running, business tools) are experiments in a portfolio, not commitments.
- No clear first offer, no clear public output channel decided.

**What he's not doing (deliberately):**

- No retail-tech or AI consulting pivot.
- No forced execution during the consolidation phase.
